articles.title: The Foundations of Intersectional Feminism
articles.summary: Overview of intersectional feminism and why it is essential to reproductive rights and gender equity work.
articles.body: Intersectional feminism recognizes that people experience gender-based oppression differently depending on race, class, disability, sexuality, immigration status, and more. This explainer traces the history of intersectional thought, highlights key scholars and activists, and outlines how our organization applies an intersectional lens to campaigns on abortion access, family leave, and trans healthcare...

articles.title: Intersectional Feminism in Reproductive Justice
articles.summary: How an intersectional lens reshapes campaigns for abortion access, contraception, and parenting with dignity.
articles.body: Reproductive justice, coined by Black women in the 1990s, expands the conversation beyond legal abortion to include the right to parent in safe communities and to bodily autonomy for all genders. This article explores case studies where intersectional organizing has changed policy outcomes...

articles.title: How Race and Disability Shape Gender Justice
articles.summary: A deeper look at how racism and ableism intersect with sexism in health care systems.
articles.body: Disabled people of color face compounded barriers in accessing reproductive and gender-affirming care. From provider bias to inaccessible clinics, these barriers are structural—not individual. This piece breaks down data on disparities and offers policy recommendations...

articles.title: Building Intersectional Movements for Abortion Access
articles.summary: Strategies for coalitions that center communities most harmed by abortion bans.
articles.body: Coalitions that center Black, Indigenous, undocumented, low-income, and trans communities build more powerful campaigns for abortion access. This article outlines organizing models, messaging tips, and real-world examples from recent ballot initiatives...

articles.title: Trans Healthcare 101: Your Rights and Options
articles.summary: A beginner-friendly guide to understanding trans-affirming healthcare and patient rights.
articles.body: Trans and nonbinary people have the right to respectful, affirming healthcare—but state laws and provider bias can make that difficult. This guide explains terminology, common types of gender-affirming care, insurance basics, and how to self-advocate at appointments...

articles.title: Barriers to Trans-Affirming Care in the South
articles.summary: How hostile state laws and provider shortages limit access to gender-affirming care across the U.S. South.
articles.body: Across the South, trans people face clinic closures, insurance exclusions, and criminalization of providers. Drawing on interviews and new research, this piece maps the landscape of attacks and highlights grassroots resistance...

articles.title: Policy Tracker: State Attacks on Gender-Affirming Care
articles.summary: A continuously updated explainer of legislation targeting gender-affirming care nationwide.
articles.body: This policy explainer summarizes current and proposed state bills that restrict gender-affirming care, with a focus on youth but including adult care where applicable. It includes a state-by-state map, timelines, and suggested actions you can take...

articles.title: Supporting Trans Youth in Healthcare Settings
articles.summary: Practical tips for caregivers and providers to create affirming healthcare experiences for trans youth.
articles.body: Trans youth deserve care that respects their names, pronouns, and autonomy. This article offers guidance on navigating pediatric visits, mental health services, and school-based health centers...

articles.title: Family Leave Basics: What Federal Law Provides
articles.summary: A plain-language explainer of the Family and Medical Leave Act and who it covers.
articles.body: The Family and Medical Leave Act (FMLA) offers important protections—but it doesn’t cover everyone. This policy explainer breaks down eligibility, what counts as covered leave, how job protection works, and where state laws go further...

articles.title: Parental Leave Policies by State: 2025 Update
articles.summary: Compare paid and unpaid parental leave laws across all 50 states and D.C.
articles.body: States are filling the gaps in federal family leave laws with their own programs—often funded through payroll contributions. This explainer includes an interactive table of state policies, benefit levels, eligibility, and implementation dates...

articles.title: Why Paid Family Leave is a Gender Equity Issue
articles.summary: Exploring how equitable family leave policies advance gender justice at work and at home.
articles.body: When caregiving falls disproportionately on women and femmes, workplace inequality widens. Paid family leave can reduce the motherhood wage penalty and encourage men to take on more caregiving, but only if policies are designed equitably...

articles.title: Quick Guide: Navigating FMLA for New Parents
articles.summary: A step-by-step checklist for new parents planning unpaid family leave under FMLA.
articles.body: This quick guide walks you through confirming eligibility, notifying your employer, gathering documentation, and planning your return to work. It includes sample email templates and a timeline you can customize...

articles.title: Understanding Reproductive Justice Beyond Abortion
articles.summary: How reproductive justice connects abortion access to housing, policing, and economic justice.
articles.body: Reproductive justice is about the right to have children, not have children, and raise the children we have in safe and sustainable communities. This article traces the framework’s origins and shows how it guides our campaigns today...

articles.title: Gender Pay Gap Myths vs. Facts
articles.summary: Debunking common myths about the gender pay gap with data and context.
articles.body: Despite persistent myths, the gender pay gap is real—and wider for Black, Latina, Indigenous, and disabled women and for many trans and nonbinary workers. This fact-driven article breaks down what the gap measures and what it leaves out...

articles.title: How Abortion Bans Hurt Marginalized Communities First
articles.summary: Examining the unequal impacts of abortion bans on low-income people, people of color, and trans communities.
articles.body: Abortion bans do not stop abortions; they only make them less safe and less accessible—especially for people already facing barriers to healthcare. This article connects abortion restrictions to policing, immigration enforcement, and economic inequality...

articles.title: Campus Toolkit: Starting a Repro Justice Group
articles.summary: A practical guide for students organizing for reproductive rights and gender equity on campus.
articles.body: From finding co-organizers to planning your first event, this guide covers the basics of building a sustainable, intersectional campus group. It includes sample bylaws, meeting agendas, and campaign ideas...

clinics.name: NYC Reproductive Health Center
clinics.description: Full-spectrum reproductive health clinic offering abortion counseling, procedures, contraception, and gender-affirming care.

clinics.name: Chelsea Women's Clinic
clinics.description: Community-based clinic providing abortion counseling, contraceptive care, and STI testing.

clinics.name: Midtown Family Planning & Wellness
clinics.description: Reproductive health and family planning services with weekend hours and sliding-scale fees.

clinics.name: Brooklyn Repro Care Collective
clinics.description: Collective clinic offering abortion services, doula support, and gender-affirming hormone care.

clinics.name: Queens Family Health Center
clinics.description: Primary and reproductive healthcare with interpreters available in multiple languages.

clinics.name: Harlem Gender-Affirming Health Clinic
clinics.description: Gender-affirming primary care, hormones, and reproductive health for trans and nonbinary people.

clinics.name: Atlanta Reproductive Health Center
clinics.description: Comprehensive reproductive healthcare, including abortion care and contraception.

clinics.name: Atlanta LGBTQ Health Collective
clinics.description: Community health center focused on LGBTQ+ primary and reproductive care.

clinics.name: Chicago Women's Health & Choice Center
clinics.description: Abortion services, contraceptive counseling, and STI testing in downtown Chicago.

clinics.name: Phoenix Repro Care & Wellness
clinics.description: Clinic providing contraception, abortion counseling via telehealth, and pregnancy options counseling.

donation_campaigns.name: Reproductive Justice Fund
donation_campaigns.short_description: Support grassroots groups advancing reproductive justice for marginalized communities.
donation_campaigns.long_description: Your gift to the Reproductive Justice Fund powers clinic escorts, abortion practical support networks, and organizing led by Black, Indigenous, immigrant, disabled, and queer communities most impacted by bans and barriers.

donation_campaigns.name: Abortion Access Defense Fund
donation_campaigns.short_description: Keep abortion accessible through legal defense, travel support, and clinic security.
donation_campaigns.long_description: Contributions to the Abortion Access Defense Fund help defend clinics from targeted harassment, support legal challenges to bans, and cover travel and lodging for patients forced to cross state lines.

donation_campaigns.name: Gender Pay Equity Fund
donation_campaigns.short_description: Advance policies and campaigns to close the gender and racial pay gaps.
donation_campaigns.long_description: The Gender Pay Equity Fund supports research, storytelling, and advocacy to win pay transparency laws, strengthen enforcement against discrimination, and uplift workers organizing for fair wages.

donation_campaigns.name: General Support Fund
donation_campaigns.short_description: Fuel our most urgent work across all campaigns.
donation_campaigns.long_description: Unrestricted gifts allow us to respond quickly to new attacks on reproductive freedom and gender equity, invest in grassroots leaders, and keep our digital tools free for everyone.

donation_campaigns.name: Trans Healthcare Access Fund
donation_campaigns.short_description: Help trans and nonbinary people access gender-affirming and reproductive care.
donation_campaigns.long_description: This fund covers transportation, lodging, copays, and name-change fees for trans and nonbinary community members, with a focus on youth and people in hostile states.

donation_campaigns.name: Campus Organizing Mini-Grants
donation_campaigns.short_description: Seed grants for student-led reproductive justice and gender equity projects.
donation_campaigns.long_description: Your donation provides micro-grants to campus groups organizing teach-ins, abortion access campaigns, and gender-inclusive policy changes at their schools.

donation_campaigns.name: Legal Advocacy for Reproductive Rights
donation_campaigns.short_description: Back strategic litigation to protect abortion and contraception access.
donation_campaigns.long_description: This campaign funds impact litigation, amicus briefs, and legal support for on-the-ground organizers in key court cases related to abortion and contraception access.

donation_campaigns.name: Working Parents Support & Family Leave Fund
donation_campaigns.short_description: Support campaigns for paid family and medical leave for all workers.
donation_campaigns.long_description: Donations help fund research, storytelling, and coalition advocacy to win comprehensive paid family and medical leave at the state and federal levels.

events.title: Online Teach-In: Repro Justice 101
events.short_description: A live online primer on reproductive justice and current attacks on abortion access.
events.long_description: Join our educators for a 60-minute interactive webinar covering the basics of reproductive justice, how it differs from reproductive rights, and what’s at stake in 2026. Includes breakout rooms for local action planning.

events.title: Webinar: Abortion Facts vs. Myths
events.short_description: A data-driven, stigma-busting online session about abortion care in the U.S.
events.long_description: In this 90-minute online webinar, our policy team walks through the latest research on abortion safety, common myths, and how to talk about abortion access with friends, family, and lawmakers.

events.title: Digital Storytelling for Gender Equity
events.short_description: Learn how to share your story online to advance gender equity campaigns.
events.long_description: This 120-minute training supports advocates in crafting and sharing stories about pay equity, family leave, and caregiving. Facilitators will offer feedback and highlight digital security best practices.

events.title: Town Hall: Defending Trans Healthcare
events.short_description: A virtual town hall on the fight for trans-affirming care nationwide.
events.long_description: Hear from trans organizers, clinicians, and legal experts about current threats to gender-affirming care and how you can plug into local and national campaigns. Includes Q&A and action breakout rooms.

events.title: In-Person Canvass: Abortion Access in Chelsea
events.short_description: Door-to-door canvass in ZIP 10001 to talk with neighbors about abortion access.
events.long_description: We’ll meet for a brief training before pairing up to knock doors around Chelsea and talk with neighbors about protecting abortion access in New York and nationally. No prior experience needed.

events.title: Rally for Gender Pay Equity
events.short_description: Join us on the steps of City Hall to demand equal pay and paid family leave.
events.long_description: This 2-hour rally will feature local workers, union leaders, and community organizations calling for robust gender pay equity legislation and strong paid leave protections.

events.title: In-Person Phonebank: Call GA Lawmakers for Repro Rights
events.short_description: Join fellow Atlantans to call state and federal representatives about reproductive rights.
events.long_description: We’ll gather in downtown Atlanta for a quick training before making calls to voters and lawmakers about key reproductive rights bills in Georgia and Congress. Scripts and snacks provided.

events.title: Hybrid Policy Briefing: Family Leave in 2026
events.short_description: An in-person and livestreamed briefing on the state of family leave policy.
events.long_description: Our policy team will present the latest research and legislative updates on paid family and medical leave, with both in-person seating and a live virtual option.

events.title: Online Training: Winning with Petitions
events.short_description: Learn how to create and promote high-impact petitions on reproductive rights and gender equity.
events.long_description: This training covers how to choose a target, write a compelling petition, and move people from signing to deeper action.

events.title: Clinic Escort Training (NYC)
events.short_description: Prepare to safely support patients entering clinics in New York City.
events.long_description: In this in-person training, experienced clinic escorts will cover safety protocols, de-escalation skills, and legal rights when supporting patients outside clinics.

events.title: Intro to Intersectional Feminism
events.short_description: A recorded webinar on intersectional feminism and organizing.
events.long_description: This past webinar recording provides a foundational overview of intersectional feminism and how to apply it to your campus or community organizing.

events.title: Community Forum: Trans Healthcare in Our City
events.short_description: Local organizers and providers discuss the state of trans healthcare access.
events.long_description: This in-person forum brought together trans community members, providers, and policymakers to discuss barriers and opportunities for improving access to gender-affirming care.

resources.title: Social Graphic: Protect Trans Healthcare

resources.title: Social Graphic: Close the Gender Pay Gap
resources.summary: Infographic highlighting racial and gender pay gaps.

resources.title: Call Script: Protect Abortion Access
resources.summary: Script for calling your representatives to oppose abortion bans and restrictions.

resources.title: Call Script: Pass Comprehensive Paid Family Leave
resources.summary: Talking points and script for urging lawmakers to support robust paid family and medical leave.

resources.title: Call Script: Defend Trans Healthcare in Georgia
resources.summary: Georgia-specific script for calling state legislators about gender-affirming care bills.

resources.title: Gender Pay Gap Fact Sheet
resources.summary: One-page overview of the gender and racial pay gaps with key statistics.

resources.title: One-Pager: Intersectional Feminism Basics
resources.summary: Quick reference explaining intersectional feminism and key terms.

resources.title: Fact Sheet: Trans Healthcare Rights
resources.summary: Key rights and protections for trans and nonbinary people seeking healthcare.

resources.title: Fact Sheet: State Paid Family Leave Programs
resources.summary: Snapshot of state-level paid family and medical leave programs across the U.S.

resources.title: Toolkit Guide: Campus Advocacy for Repro Justice
resources.summary: Step-by-step guide for building a campus reproductive justice campaign.

resources.title: Toolkit Guide: Organizing Online Actions
resources.summary: Guide to hosting effective online rallies, town halls, and trainings.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Remote Abortion Rights Hotline Volunteer
volunteer_opportunities.short_description: Answer non-medical hotline calls to connect people with abortion resources.
volunteer_opportunities.full_description: Volunteers will provide compassionate, non-judgmental information on abortion access, funding resources, and logistics. Training and supervision provided; no medical advice required.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Digital Outreach for Gender Equity Campaign
volunteer_opportunities.short_description: Help run social media and texting outreach for our gender pay equity campaign.
volunteer_opportunities.full_description: Volunteers will send texts, respond to social media comments using our guidelines, and help test messages that move people to take action on pay equity and paid leave.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Remote Research Assistant: Trans Healthcare Policy
volunteer_opportunities.short_description: Track and summarize state bills affecting trans healthcare.
volunteer_opportunities.full_description: Volunteers will monitor legislative databases, summarize bill language, and update our internal policy tracker to support rapid-response advocacy.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Remote Blog Contributor: Intersectional Feminism
volunteer_opportunities.short_description: Write short blog posts highlighting intersectional organizing.
volunteer_opportunities.full_description: Contributors will pitch and draft 800–1,000 word posts about campaigns at the intersections of race, disability, gender, and class, working with our editorial team.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Remote Phonebank: Paid Family Leave
volunteer_opportunities.short_description: Call voters in key districts to build support for paid family and medical leave.
volunteer_opportunities.full_description: Using our dialer system and scripts, volunteers will call voters to share information about family leave campaigns and connect supporters to their lawmakers.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Clinic Escort (NYC)
volunteer_opportunities.short_description: Support patients entering clinics in Manhattan and Brooklyn.
volunteer_opportunities.full_description: In-person volunteers stand outside partner clinics to welcome patients, provide directions, and help de-escalate harassment alongside trained team leads.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Event Support: Rallies & Marches
volunteer_opportunities.short_description: Help with logistics at in-person rallies for reproductive rights.
volunteer_opportunities.full_description: Tasks include set-up, sign distribution, greeting participants, and accessibility support such as guiding attendees and distributing water.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Remote Data Entry: Donation Processing
volunteer_opportunities.short_description: Support our fundraising team by helping with data entry.
volunteer_opportunities.full_description: Volunteers will accurately enter donation records into our database, help with quality checks, and flag discrepancies for staff follow-up.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Remote Social Media Moderator
volunteer_opportunities.short_description: Help keep our online spaces safe by moderating comments and DMs.
volunteer_opportunities.full_description: Moderators will review comments for harassment and misinformation, apply our moderation guidelines, and escalate sensitive content to staff.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Remote Textbank: Defend Trans Healthcare
volunteer_opportunities.short_description: Send texts to supporters about actions to protect trans healthcare.
volunteer_opportunities.full_description: Using our peer-to-peer texting platform, volunteers will send and respond to messages encouraging supporters to attend town halls, contact lawmakers, and share stories.

volunteer_opportunities.title: In-Person Volunteer Team Lead
volunteer_opportunities.short_description: Coordinate volunteers at local events and canvasses.
volunteer_opportunities.full_description: Team leads help onboard new volunteers, manage sign-in, troubleshoot logistics, and debrief after events to improve future actions.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Remote Host Committee: Online Fundraiser
volunteer_opportunities.short_description: Help plan and promote our spring online fundraiser.
volunteer_opportunities.full_description: Host committee members will invite their networks, co-host the livestream, and support live donation asks.

rep_email_templates.title: Share Your Views on Abortion Access
rep_email_templates.body:
Dear Sen. Jones,

I am a constituent writing from ZIP 30301 to share my views on abortion access. I believe decisions about pregnancy should be made by individuals in consultation with their healthcare providers, not politicians.

As Congress considers legislation related to abortion care, I urge you to take into account the perspectives of people in our community and the real-world impacts your votes will have on families here in Georgia.

Thank you for your time and service.

Sincerely,
[YOUR NAME]
[YOUR ADDRESS]
[YOUR CITY], GA [YOUR ZIP]

rep_email_templates.title: Urge a Strong Vote for Abortion Access
rep_email_templates.body:
Dear Sen. Jones,

As your constituent in ZIP 30301, I am asking you to strongly support abortion access and oppose any legislation that restricts safe, legal abortion care.

People in Georgia are already facing enormous barriers to reproductive healthcare, from clinic closures to financial and logistical hurdles. We need you to fight for policies that protect access to abortion in every state, ensure insurance coverage, and safeguard patients and providers from criminalization.

Please commit to:
- Voting against any bill that bans or further restricts abortion care;
- Supporting legislation that codifies the right to abortion in federal law; and
- Publicly speaking out against misinformation and stigma about abortion.

Your leadership on this issue will save lives and protect our fundamental rights.

Sincerely,
[YOUR NAME]
[YOUR ADDRESS]
[YOUR CITY], GA [YOUR ZIP]

rep_email_templates.title: Support Comprehensive Reproductive Rights
rep_email_templates.body:
Dear Sen. Jones,

I am a constituent in ZIP 30301, and I am writing to urge you to support comprehensive reproductive rights, including access to contraception, abortion care, safe pregnancy, and parenting with dignity.

Policies that restrict reproductive healthcare deepen racial and economic inequality and put the health and lives of pregnant people at risk. I ask that you:
- Protect funding for reproductive healthcare services;
- Oppose efforts to criminalize patients, providers, or those who help people access care; and
- Support evidence-based, patient-centered policies grounded in medical standards.

Thank you for considering my views on this critical issue.

Sincerely,
[YOUR NAME]
[YOUR ADDRESS]
[YOUR CITY], GA [YOUR ZIP]

rep_email_templates.title: Demand Strong Protection for Abortion Rights
rep_email_templates.body:
Dear Sen. Jones,

As your constituent in ZIP 30301, I am demanding that you be a champion for abortion rights and reproductive freedom at every opportunity.

Abortion is essential healthcare and a fundamental human right. Politicians should never force someone to remain pregnant against their will. I urge you to:

- Publicly and unequivocally affirm that you support the legal right to abortion in all states;
- Cosponsor and vote for federal legislation that restores and expands abortion access nationwide, free from medically unnecessary restrictions;
- Oppose any bill, amendment, or budget rider that limits abortion care, criminalizes patients or providers, or allows states to ban abortion; and
- Use your platform to challenge stigma and misinformation about abortion and to center the voices of those most harmed by bans, including Black, Brown, Indigenous, immigrant, low-income, disabled, and trans communities.

Our freedoms and our lives are on the line. I expect you to lead with courage and make the strongest possible defense of abortion rights.

Sincerely,
[YOUR NAME]
[YOUR ADDRESS]
[YOUR CITY], GA [YOUR ZIP]

rep_email_templates.title: Protect Safe and Legal Abortion Access
rep_email_templates.body:
Dear [OFFICE HOLDER TITLE] [LAST NAME],

I am a constituent writing to urge you to protect safe and legal access to abortion care.

Every person should have the freedom to decide whether and when to be pregnant, without political interference. Abortion bans and medically unnecessary restrictions do not make people safer; they force people to travel long distances, delay care, or carry pregnancies against their will.

Please:
- Oppose any abortion bans or restrictions in our state and at the federal level;
- Support policies that expand access, including coverage for abortion in public and private insurance;
- Ensure that patients, providers, and helpers are not criminalized for seeking or providing care.

I will be watching your leadership on this issue and voting accordingly.

Sincerely,
[YOUR NAME]
[YOUR ADDRESS]
[YOUR CITY], [YOUR STATE] [YOUR ZIP]

rep_email_templates.title: Oppose Attacks on Trans-Affirming Healthcare
rep_email_templates.body:
Dear Rep. Lopez,

As your constituent in Georgia, I am calling on you to strongly oppose any legislation that restricts or criminalizes gender-affirming healthcare for trans and nonbinary people.

Major medical organizations agree that gender-affirming care is evidence-based, life-saving healthcare. Bills that ban or limit this care put trans youth and adults at risk of serious harm.

Please commit to:
- Voting against any bill that limits access to gender-affirming care or targets providers;
- Publicly affirming your support for the rights and dignity of trans and nonbinary people;
- Working with community organizations to expand access to culturally competent, affirming care in Georgia.

Trans people in our state deserve safety, respect, and medically necessary care.

Sincerely,
[YOUR NAME]
[YOUR ADDRESS]
[YOUR CITY], GA [YOUR ZIP]

rep_email_templates.title: Defend Trans Healthcare Without Compromise
rep_email_templates.body:
Dear Sen. Lee,

I live in your district and am urging you to be an outspoken, unwavering defender of trans healthcare in Georgia.

Gender-affirming care is medically necessary, supported by decades of research, and endorsed by every major medical association. Extremist attacks on trans people are costing lives and driving families to leave our state.

I ask you to:
- Forcefully oppose any anti-trans legislation, including bans on gender-affirming care or restrictions on name and gender-marker changes;
- Introduce and support proactive bills that protect access to trans-affirming care and shield providers and families from harassment and criminalization;
- Use your platform to counter hateful rhetoric and affirm that trans people belong in Georgia.

Our communities need you to take the strongest possible stand for trans rights and healthcare.

Sincerely,
[YOUR NAME]
[YOUR ADDRESS]
[YOUR CITY], GA [YOUR ZIP]

rep_email_templates.title: Support Paid Family and Medical Leave in Georgia
rep_email_templates.body:
Dear Rep. White,

As a resident of your district, I am writing to ask you to support comprehensive paid family and medical leave for workers in Georgia.

Too many families are forced to choose between a paycheck and caring for a new child, a serious health condition, or an aging parent. Paid leave policies improve health outcomes, keep people attached to the workforce, and advance gender equity.

Please:
- Support legislation that creates a statewide paid family and medical leave program that covers all kinds of families and all genders;
- Ensure benefits are accessible to low-wage workers and small business employees;
- Oppose any efforts to weaken or narrow proposed paid leave protections.

Georgia families are counting on your leadership.

Sincerely,
[YOUR NAME]
[YOUR ADDRESS]
[YOUR CITY], GA [YOUR ZIP]

rep_email_templates.title: Advance Bold Gender Equity Legislation
rep_email_templates.body:
Dear Sen. Morales,

As a New York resident, I am urging you to continue championing bold gender equity policies that address the intersecting impacts of sexism, racism, and economic injustice.

We need laws that:
- Close gender and racial pay gaps through pay transparency, stronger enforcement, and support for workers who face retaliation;
- Guarantee robust paid family and medical leave, including protections for caregivers of all genders;
- Expand protections from harassment and discrimination for women, femmes, and LGBTQ+ people in the workplace and beyond.

Please use your position to ensure that New York leads the country on gender equity.

Sincerely,
[YOUR NAME]
[YOUR ADDRESS]
[YOUR CITY], NY [YOUR ZIP]

rep_email_templates.title: Strengthen New York's Paid Family Leave
rep_email_templates.body:
Dear Rep. Kim,

I live in ZIP 10001 and am proud that New York has a paid family leave program, but we must go further to ensure everyone can care for themselves and their loved ones.

I urge you to:
- Support legislation that increases wage replacement rates so low-wage workers can actually afford to use leave;
- Expand job protections to cover more workers, including those in non-traditional and gig employment;
- Ensure the program is inclusive of all family structures and caregiving relationships.

Paid family and medical leave is essential infrastructure for families and a critical gender equity policy.

Sincerely,
[YOUR NAME]
[YOUR ADDRESS]
New York, NY [YOUR ZIP]

rep_email_templates.title: Address the Gender Pay Gap and Workplace Inequity
rep_email_templates.body:
Dear [OFFICE HOLDER TITLE] [LAST NAME],

I am writing as your constituent to ask you to address persistent gender and racial inequities in our workplaces.

Womenespecially Black, Latina, Indigenous, disabled, and many trans and nonbinary workerscontinue to be paid less than white, non-disabled men and are more likely to experience harassment, discrimination, and retaliation.

Please support policies that:
- Strengthen equal pay protections and transparency;
- Expand paid family and medical leave and affordable childcare;
- Improve enforcement against harassment and discrimination.

Our community needs bold action to ensure everyone can work with dignity and fairness.

Sincerely,
[YOUR NAME]
[YOUR ADDRESS]
[YOUR CITY], [YOUR STATE] [YOUR ZIP]

rep_email_templates.title: Stand Up for Trans-Affirming Healthcare
rep_email_templates.body:
Dear [OFFICE HOLDER TITLE] [LAST NAME],

I am your constituent, and I am asking you to stand up for the rights of trans and nonbinary people to access affirming, evidence-based healthcare.

Attacks on trans healthcare are out of step with medical science and human rights. Denying or delaying gender-affirming care increases depression, anxiety, and suicide risk, especially among youth.

Please:
- Oppose any legislation that restricts access to gender-affirming care or targets providers and families;
- Support policies that expand access to affordable, culturally competent care;
- Publicly affirm that trans and nonbinary people deserve respect, safety, and the healthcare they need.

Your leadership can help protect some of the most marginalized members of our community.

Sincerely,
[YOUR NAME]
[YOUR ADDRESS]
[YOUR CITY], [YOUR STATE] [YOUR ZIP]

rep_email_templates.title: Defend Reproductive Freedom for All
rep_email_templates.body:
Dear [OFFICE HOLDER TITLE] [LAST NAME],

I am your constituent, and I urge you to defend reproductive freedom for all people in our community.

Reproductive justice means the right to have children, not have children, and raise the children we have in safe and sustainable communities. That requires access to abortion, contraception, prenatal care, and the resources needed to parent with dignity.

I ask you to:
- Protect and expand access to the full range of reproductive healthcare services;
- Oppose criminalization of pregnancy outcomes and people who self-manage abortions;
- Support policies that address the economic, racial, and gender inequities that shape reproductive decisions.

Please act decisively to advance reproductive freedom.

Sincerely,
[YOUR NAME]
[YOUR ADDRESS]
[YOUR CITY], [YOUR STATE] [YOUR ZIP]

petitions.title: Protect Abortion Access Nationwide with a Federal Guarantee
petitions.short_description: Tell Congress to pass a federal law restoring and expanding abortion access in every state.

petitions.title: Pass Strong State Shield Laws for Abortion Patients and Providers
petitions.short_description: Urge state lawmakers to protect patients, providers, and helpers from out-of-state abortion bans.

petitions.title: End Abortion Coverage Bans in Medicaid and Public Insurance
petitions.short_description: Tell Congress to repeal federal bans on abortion coverage and ensure everyone can afford care.

petitions.title: Add Reproductive Freedom to Our State Constitution
petitions.short_description: Demand a constitutional amendment that protects abortion, contraception, and pregnancy care.

petitions.title: Pass Comprehensive Gender Pay Equity and Fair Work Laws
petitions.short_description: Urge lawmakers to close the gender and racial pay gaps with strong transparency and enforcement.

petitions.title: Require Salary Ranges in All Job Postings
petitions.short_description: Tell your state legislature: workers deserve pay transparency to fight wage discrimination.

petitions.title: Protect Title IX and LGBTQ+ Students from Discrimination
petitions.short_description: Defend Title IX from rollbacks and ensure explicit protections for LGBTQ+ students.

petitions.title: Stop Bans on Gender-Affirming Care for Trans Youth
petitions.short_description: Tell lawmakers to reject cruel bans that deny trans youth life-saving healthcare.

petitions.title: Reinstate and Strengthen Federal Protections for Trans Healthcare
petitions.short_description: Demand robust non-discrimination protections so trans people can access healthcare without fear.

petitions.title: Create a National Paid Family and Medical Leave Program
petitions.short_description: Tell Congress: every worker deserves paid time to care for themselves and their loved ones.

petitions.title: Guarantee Paid Leave for Gig and Contract Workers
petitions.short_description: Close the loopholes that leave gig, temp, and contract workers without paid leave.

petitions.title: Provide Free Pregnancy Tests and Options Counseling on Campus
petitions.short_description: Ask your university to offer free, confidential pregnancy tests and unbiased counseling.
